sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a 38-year-old male, presented to the office with a complaint of right knee pain after a recent incident at a trampoline park, where he heard a "snap" and experienced instant pain.
He reports that this is the first time he has engaged in this type of activity and that he does not regularly exercise.
He has been trying to elevate the knee and has taken Tylenol for the pain, but it has not provided significant relief.
He has a history of hypertension, for which he is currently taking 20 milligrams of lisinopril, and diabetes, for which he is taking metformin 500 milligrams.
He reports that his blood sugars have been a bit better lately, but he has not been checking them as frequently as recommended.
He has not been monitoring his blood pressure or taking his medication as directed.
Review of systems:
The patient reports no other symptoms or complaints.
Current medications:
I prescribed lisinopril 20 milligrams daily for hypertension and metformin 500 milligrams daily for diabetes.
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 180/80 mmHg
Pulse rate: not mentioned
Oxygen saturation: not mentioned
Temperature: not mentioned
Physical exam:
The patient has a slight limp when walking.
There is ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee.
The patient has a decent range of motion, but experiences pain with some movement.
The knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Test Results:
The results of the right knee x-ray are as follows: no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Right knee pain; hypertension; diabetes; suspected blood pressure issue; unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 milligrams daily for pain and swelling related to the lateral ligament strain in the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20 milligrams daily for hypertension.
I ordered an echo to assess the patient's cardiac function and to determine if medication modification is necessary.
I also ordered an A1c and a lipid panel to further evaluate the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up:
The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the echo, A1c, and lipid panel, and to assess the progress of the lateral ligament strain in the right knee.
